allies.rationale_text: The Feynman Technique is the operational antidote to the curse of knowledge — it forces the expert to translate their understanding into language accessible to a person with no prior exposure, exposing exactly where the curse was operating.
allies.source_quote: The Feynman Technique is the operational antidote to the curse of knowledge: it forces the expert to translate understanding into language accessible to a person with no prior exposure.

allies.rationale_text: Cognitive Load Theory provides the recipient-side model that curse-of-knowledge ignores — working memory is finite, and curse-afflicted communication regularly exceeds it by delivering compressed, unfamiliar content simultaneously.
allies.source_quote: CLT provides the recipient-side model: working memory is finite, and expert communication regularly exceeds it by delivering too much compressed, unfamiliar content simultaneously.

allies.rationale_text: Expertise-reversal-effect provides a complementary lens: while curse-of-knowledge describes the expert's failure to serve novices, ERE describes how overly scaffolded instruction can backfire with experts — together they define the adaptive communication challenge.
allies.source_quote: ERE describes when instructional methods optimized for novices actually harm experts; CoK is the inverse failure: communication optimized for experts that fails novices.

antagonists.rationale_text: Empathy is the disposition that, when activated, motivates the expert to genuinely attempt to model the novice's state — but the curse of knowledge structurally impairs the simulation even when the motivation is present.
antagonists.source_quote: Empathy is the motivational counterpart to the cognitive failure: CoK makes the simulation difficult; empathy makes it worth trying.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Curse-of-knowledge conflicts with feynman-technique when expert fluency suppresses the deliberate reconstruction of understanding from first principles that the Feynman standard demands.
structured_tensions.source_quote: curse-of-knowledge vs feynman-technique: curse-of-knowledge conflicts with feynman-technique (Feynman technique) when expert fluency suppresses the deliberate reconstruction of understanding from first principles.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Curse-of-knowledge conflicts with cognitive-load-theory when expert communication ignores working memory constraints by delivering compressed, jargon-dense content that exceeds novice processing capacity.
structured_tensions.source_quote: curse-of-knowledge vs cognitive-load-theory: curse-of-knowledge conflicts with cognitive-load-theory (cognitive load theory) when expert communication ignores working memory constraints.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Curse-of-knowledge conflicts with scaffolding when the expert's automated knowledge makes the necessary scaffolding gaps invisible, producing instruction that begins above the learner's actual starting point.
structured_tensions.source_quote: curse-of-knowledge vs scaffolding: curse-of-knowledge conflicts with scaffolding (scaffolding) when the expert's automated knowledge makes the necessary scaffolding gaps invisible.

curation_notes.summary: Curse-of-knowledge Wave 3 curated from the new source markdown. Key allies: feynman-technique (operational antidote), cognitive-load-theory (recipient-side model), expertise-reversal-effect (complementary lens). Key tensions: feynman-technique, cognitive-load-theory, scaffolding.
open_questions: Empathy wired as antagonist with medium confidence — the relation is real but the directionality is subtle: empathy motivates the attempt but does not overcome the structural simulation failure.
